macro_segments.title: Workers' Rights to Eat: Stipulation and the Four Categories of Non-Entitled Workers

micro_segments.title: Mishnah on stipulation: Who can and cannot waive eating rights
Logic of the mishnah: Da'at as basis for waiving rights
Animal exemption: Muzzling prohibition independent of wage negotiation

macro_segments.title: Fourth-Year Produce and Broken/Reopened Containers: When Workers Cannot Eat

micro_segments.title: Mishnah cases: Fourth-year produce and lack of disclosure
Broken and reopened containers: Ma'aser obligation triggers non-entitlement

macro_segments.title: Fruit Watchers (Shomrei Peirot): Torah Law vs. Communal Practice (Hilchos Medina)

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Shomrei peirot eat by practice but not halachically
Rav's position: Watching is like labor; attachment stage determines entitlement
Shmuel's position: Watching is not real labor; entitlement limited to post-harvest
Visual diagram: Timeline of attachment, harvest, and gmar melacha stages
Gemara challenges: Parah adumah guarding and cucumber fields
Rav Ashi's reasoning: Shmuel's position fits the Mishnah's inferential logic
Conclusion: Shmuel prevails; non-labor watching yields only communal-practice rights

macro_segments.title: Four Types of Watchers (Shomrim): Liability Framework and Negligence Standards

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Four categories of shomrim and their liability levels
Gemara: Author of Mishnah; three halachic categories despite four roles
Dispute over socher (renter) classification: Rav Meir vs. Rabbi Yehuda

macro_segments.title: Paid Watcher's Obligation: Norms of Care and Contextual Standards (Rabba's Radical Rule)

micro_segments.title: Case: Shepherd by river; one sheep falls in water
Rabba's ruling: Watched like normal people watch—contextual standard prevails
Abaye's objection: Time-based breaks (lunch, siesta) still expected
Gemara challenges Rabba with tannaitic sources on extra vigilance
Bar Adda case: Single-file protection—between norm and theoretical maximum
Rav Papa's response: Norms persist, but liability persists if higher standard rejected

macro_segments.title: Theft, Reimbursement, and Responsibility: Free vs. Paid Watcher Obligations

micro_segments.title: Case: Flax stolen by armed thief; later thief apprehended
Rav Nachman's ruling: Watcher pays owner, recovers from thief
Rav Huna bar Avin: Free watcher may choose oath; paid watcher must recover
Rabba's exception: Negligence (armed bandits available) overrides ones exemption

macro_segments.title: What Constitutes Ones (Force Majeure): Wild Animals, Bandits, and Natural Death

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Ones vs. non-ones—wolves, dogs, predators, bandits, natural death
One wolf non-ones; two wolves is ones; R. Yehuda: wolf-running season makes one ones
Bandits: Armed bandits are ones; unarmed one-on-one is not necessarily ones
Shepherd equally armed: Still not required to fight; robber assumes risk, shepherd does not
Irresponsible disclosure to robber: Shepherd tipped off location—treated as negligence

topical_tags.term: Da'at (intelligence/will) as criterion for waiving rights
Mechubar (attached to ground) vs. Taloosh (harvested)
Gmar melacha (completion of work/obligation in tithes)
Hilchos medina (norms/practices of the land)
Mishmar k'oseh ma'aseh dami (watching as equivalent to labor)
Shomer chinam (free watcher): lowest liability, swears he is not negligent
Shomer sachar / Nosei sachar (paid watcher): intermediate liability
Hashoel (borrower): highest liability, pays even for ones (force majeure)
Socher (renter): liability parallel to paid watcher
Natri k'natri inshi (watches like normal people watch)
Ones (force majeure/unpreventable accident)
Pshia / Pashea (negligence, breach of duty)
T'chilato bipshiah v'sofo b'ones (begins with negligence, ends with ones)
Gneiva v'Aveida (theft and loss)
Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Yehuda on liability categories
Rav (Babylonian amora) on watching equivalence
Shmuel (Babylonian amora) on watching as non-labor
Rabba (Babylonian amora) on contextual negligence standards
Abaye challenging Rabba on norms and time-based breaks
Rav Papa on armed shepherd vs. armed bandit liability
Rav Ashi on Shmuel's logical fit with Mishnah
Rav Huna bar Avin on free vs. paid watcher options in theft
Bar Adda on single-file animal passage and liability
Parah adumah (red heifer) impurity from watching
Ganeiva purtzta (stealthy theft)
Lifnim mishurat hadin (beyond the letter of the law)
Zeev / Zeevim (wolf/wolves) threshold for ones
Listim / Listim mezuyan (bandits, armed bandits)
